title: mock.health round artifact (v1)
description: Single-file conformance or benchmark round. One per (round_id, kind). Frontend imports this directly; publisher writes it; AEGIS runner produces the conformance variant.

image.description: Container image reference exactly as it appears in docker-compose.yml (e.g. 'hapiproject/hapi:latest'). Pinning to sha256 digests is tracked separately.

image_digest.description: Runtime-captured sha256:... of the image that actually ran. Optional — populated when the round-gen pipeline runs `docker image inspect` after a test run.

source_url.description: Canonical upstream project URL — GitHub repo for open-source servers, vendor docs for commercial ones. Used on the ServerPage header so readers can audit what was tested.

dockerfile_url.description: URL to the exact Dockerfile used when the server is built locally (e.g. forked vendor binary). Absent for servers pulled from an upstream registry.

description.description: 1-3 sentence scope statement, especially for surface/subset profiles. Surfaces in the matrix tooltip + profile detail page.

applicability.description: Optional per-profile gate: probe executed once per server before running tests. If the probe matches any na_if_* rule, the server's cell for this profile becomes N/A instead of failing.

na_if_body_contains.description: Per-substring rules: N/A if the probe response body contains `substring`. Lets us distinguish 'has a bug' from 'feature requires infra not in this deployment' when both return 5xx.

na_reason.description: Default N/A reason used when a na_if_status rule matches, or when a na_if_body_contains rule has no explicit `reason`.

status.description: grey = not-yet-tested; na = not applicable (server does not implement the profile's surface; see na_reason)

na_reason.description: Human-readable reason the cell is N/A (present iff status=na)

max_checkpoint_reached.description: Benchmark cells: largest checkpoint (patient count) the server completed this workload at. Drives the headline value shown in the matrix.

trust.description: Benchmark cells: mirrors the trust block of the headline (last) evidence row so the heatmap can decide whether to desaturate the cell without reading into evidence[].

Per-quantile trustworthiness derived from sample count, plus a headline `reliable` flag driven by error rate and throughput. Quantile q requires ~10 samples above it for a stable read, so thresholds rise with q. A quantile below its threshold is still emitted (see pXX_ms) but should be rendered with a caveat. The headline `reliable` flag is what the heatmap should read to decide whether to desaturate — a cell where the server mostly timed out produces numbers that are mathematically computable but practically misleading.

reliable.description: Headline trust flag: true iff err_rate <= 0.05 AND ok-throughput >= 1 ok/sec. When false, the heatmap desaturates the cell.

reason.description: Short human-readable reason present only when reliable=false (e.g. 'err_rate=94%', 'n_ok=58 at 0.06 ok/s').

evidence.description: Polymorphic by round.kind. Conformance rounds populate test_id/bucket/outcome; benchmark rounds populate checkpoint + pXX_ms. Field requirement is enforced by the emitter, not the schema, so a single evidence array can carry either shape.

title.description: Conformance-only: TestScript.title — human-readable heading for this test

description.description: Conformance-only: TestScript.description — prose explaining what the test validates and why it matters

spec.description: Conformance-only: structured citation into the FHIR spec / IG that this test exercises. Promoted from TestScript.relatedArtifact[0] so the UI can render each evidence row as a clickable citation.

label.description: Short human-readable section name, e.g. 'FHIR R4 §3.1 read interaction'

url.description: Deep link to the exact spec anchor on hl7.org

outcome.description: Conformance-only: na = not applicable (profile-level applicability probe failed; test was not run)

report_path.description: relative path to TestReport JSON in the round dir

checkpoint.description: Benchmark-only: ingest checkpoint (patient count) at which this evidence row was captured

p50_ms.description: Benchmark-only: 50th percentile (median) latency in milliseconds — headline metric for the heatmap and log-scaling curve (ok-only for search; all-requests for ingest/crud)

p75_ms.description: Benchmark-only: 75th percentile latency — survives small samples better than p95 (n_ok >= 40 is usually enough for ±10%). Paired with p50 lets readers see spread without demanding tail-stable samples.

p90_ms.description: Benchmark-only: 90th percentile latency — trustworthy at n_ok >= 100 (see trust.p90_trustworthy).

p95_ms.description: Benchmark-only: 95th percentile latency — tail evidence, not the headline

p99_ms.description: Benchmark-only: 99th percentile latency — tail evidence; retained for inspection but not the headline because 2-minute runs can be sample-starved on slow cells

ops_ok_per_s.description: Benchmark-only: throughput of successful responses only (ok-count / active-elapsed). The publishable-throughput signal — a server that timed out on 90% of requests should not get credit for its total request rate.

n_ok.description: Benchmark-only: count of successful (ok=true) requests backing the pXX_ms values when the headline uses ok-only stream (search); count of all records when the headline uses the full stream (CRUD).

n_err.description: Benchmark-only: count of failed (ok=false) requests, including 60s worker timeouts. A high value is a legitimate 'server could not complete the workload' signal, not a harness bug.

error_rate.description: Benchmark-only: fraction of requests that returned non-2xx (or timed out) at this checkpoint

db_size_bytes.description: Benchmark-only: sum of named-volume bytes owned by this server at cell completion (from `docker system df -v`). Includes all storage sidecars (e.g. Aidbox's Postgres, search-index sidecars). Omitted when disk.json was missing or unparseable — distinguishes 'not measured' from 'zero bytes'. Servers with bind-mounted data will read 0; only named Docker volumes are counted.

trust.description: Benchmark-only: per-quantile trustworthiness for this evidence row plus a headline `reliable` flag.

crud_phases.description: Benchmark-only, CRUD cells only: per-verb phase accounting (C/R/U/V/D/prewarm) — planned cap, actual samples, elapsed ms, and stop_reason. Lets the per-verb reliability flag be surfaced in the UI without recomputing from the jsonl.

per_verb.description: Benchmark-only: per-verb breakdown of this checkpoint. Enables chart drill-down by individual query (search) or op (CRUD: C/R/U/D). Percentiles follow the same ok-only-vs-all-requests convention as the top-level fields.

verb.description: Query ID (search) or single-letter op (CRUD: C/R/U/D)

n.description: Sample count backing this verb's percentiles
